title: 2 Terminology and Frequently Asked Questions
body_text:
2	Terminology and Frequently Asked Questions
2.1	Definitions
Account Entity ID
The Account Entity ID is your customer芒聙聶s Account ID, which can also be linked to the bank identifier (FI ID, Transit #, Account #).
Alert
An alert is generated within EFM when a payment transaction has been assessed through the decision strategy (made up of models/scores/rules) and deemed to be suspicious. An alert is associated to a payment transaction.
Analytics
Analytics encompass rules, aggregators, and models that provide analysts with information on high-risk events. Central 1 sets up the analytics for your financial institution.
Auto Risk
Auto Risk indicates that EFM automatically rejected the transaction. This occurs on any subsequent transactions when the system has created an alert for a previous transaction for that customer ID, which hasn芒聙聶t been decisioned yet.
checkDepositRT
A checkDepositRT is the event when a customer deposits a cheque, money order, draft, or Receiver General Warrant to their account at your financial institution using Deposit Anywhere on a mobile device.
Client Centre
The Client Centre is a digital portal that provides secured access to Central 1芒聙聶s applications, and to information and news about our products and services.
Client Centre Users
Client Centre Users log in to the Client Centre to access certain applications. Client Centre Users can芒聙聶t manage CPS Admin Security Officers or other Client Centre Users.
CPS Admin
The Central Portal System (CPS) Admin application is used to add, edit, and delete Client Centre Users and CPS Admin Security Officers.
CPS Admin Security Officer
CPS Admin Security Officers use the CPS Admin or the User Management application to add, edit, and delete Client Centre Users. They can also delete and modify other CPS Admin Security Officers (with some exceptions) via the User Management application. CPS Admin Security Officers use the Client Centre to log in to certain applications (e.g., Branch Capture).
CounterParty Entity ID
A CounterParty Entity ID identifies the receiver of a transaction. The CounterParty Entity provides different details based on the payment type:
Regular Interac
e-Transfer
脗庐
芒聙聯 shows recipient email and/or recipient phone
Account Number Routing (ANR)
芒聙聯 shows recipient芒聙聶s account information (Route+Transit+Account Number)
Money Request
(to become available later)
芒聙聯 shows full name of the person who is receiving the request for money
EFM
Enterprise Fraud Management (EFM) is used to monitor payments for suspicious activity related to fraud. It is comprised of a decision engine, case management and reporting or monitoring (your access may restrict what components you can see in EFM).
Entity
An entity represents an individual (your customer) or unique attribute of that customer. Each entity is identified by a unique entity ID in the event data (e.g., Account Entity ID, Customer Entity ID).
Entity ID
The Customer Entity ID is the customer芒聙聶s ID, which can also be linked to the bank identifier (FI ID, Transit#, Member#).
Event
An event can be a customer transaction, a new customer application, or a merchant attempting to process a payment. Each event is associated with one or more entities.
Incident
An incident is related to an entity (typically a customer) and comprises of one or more alerts to review.
Notification
A notification is an email sent to your fraud team when an alert is generated within EFM. The notification indicates a transaction in EFM has been assessed as suspicious and needs review.
OAS
Interac
脗庐
e-Transfer聽Online Administration System (OAS).
paymentNRT
A paymentNRT is the event when the payment is confirmed and submitted to Interac
.
Only transactions with the paymentNRT and paymentRT events can be located in OAS.
paymentRT
A paymentRT is the event when a customer initiates the payment. It indicates the real time when the Interac e-Transfer was initiated. Counterparty details (name and email address) will be added to watchlists once you identify the transaction as fraudulent.
Routing Customer ID
The Routing Customer ID is the same as the Customer Entity ID.
Rule
A rule defines some simple logic. Rules take in information from events and other data and provide a true or false response. Central 1 creates and manages the rules for your financial institution as part of the system.
Tag
A tag can be added to alerts to give users more information.
Tenant
A tenant is the financial institution using Enterprise Fraud Management (EFM).
Transaction ID
A transaction ID is the unique ID number for each transaction.
User Management
User Management is an application used to add, edit, and delete Client Centre Users. It is also used to edit and delete other CPS Admin Security Officers and to manage 2-step security tokens. Eventually, User Management will replace CPS Admin.
wireRT
A wireRT is the event when a customer initiates a wire payment. The wire can be either Inbound or Outbound.
2.2	Frequently Asked Questions
All Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs) are accessible
here
.
END
